assertions.text: Correctly classifies the chatbot as limited risk (not high-risk) under Art. 50 — not falling under Annex III
assertions.evidence: Response correctly identifies 'LIMITED/MINIMAL RISK tier, not high-risk, since product Q&A, returns processing, and browsing-based recommendations do not match Annex III high-risk categories'

assertions.text: Correctly states Art. 50(1) disclosure obligation: deployers must inform users they interact with an AI system
assertions.evidence: Response explicitly states 'Mandatory AI Disclosure — Article 50(1): YES, disclosure that it is an AI is mandatory. The chatbot must clearly inform users at the start of each interaction'

assertions.text: Notes that an e-commerce chatbot is not 'obviously AI' to a reasonably well-informed person and therefore disclosure is required
assertions.evidence: Response discusses the 'obvious from circumstances' exception and concludes that the disclosure obligation applies to chatbots by design, stating they 'cannot claim to be human'

assertions.text: Distinguishes deployer obligations (Art. 26, Art. 50) from provider/GPAI provider obligations (Art. 53)
assertions.evidence: Response correctly distinguishes: 'compliance burden for the underlying model sits primarily with the GPAI provider' and lists deployer-specific obligations separately under Art. 26 and Art. 50

assertions.text: States the 2 August 2026 application date for Art. 50 limited-risk transparency requirements
assertions.evidence: Response states 'most provisions applying from 2 August 2026' and specifically mentions Art. 50 transparency obligations in that context
